macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Ends Five-Straight Weekly Losses Amid Middle East Tensions
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell 2% for its fifth consecutive weekly loss, the first since 2022, as US-Iran tensions flared, dashing ceasefire hopes and pushing oil prices higher. US stock futures dropped 0.6-0.7% early Monday ahead of key earnings.[1][2]

items.title: Oil Prices Surge to $112/Barrel on Strait of Hormuz Uncertainty
items.summary: Brent crude climbed to $112 per barrel after Iran reopened then reportedly closed the Strait of Hormuz amid ceasefire talks, raising inflation concerns and questioning central bank rate cut plans. Elevated prices test if policy rates can keep pace with inflation.[1][2]

items.title: S&P 500 Hits Record High Above 7000 Before Pullback
items.summary: The S&P 500 rallied to all-time highs above 7000 for the first time since January, rebounding from a near-correction in just 11 days on de-escalation hopes, but futures slipped Sunday on renewed Iran tensions. Narrow market breadth persists despite records.[3]

items.title: Markets Price 99.5% Chance Fed Holds Rates Steady in April
items.summary: CME FedWatch tool shows near-certainty of unchanged Fed rates at the April meeting amid oil shock and inflation risks. US 10-year Treasury yields rose to 4.43%, with 10-year at 4.274% and two-year at 3.741% in recent trading.[1][2]

items.title: Earnings Week Kicks Off with Tesla, Intel, UnitedHealth, GE Reports
items.summary: A heavy slate of corporate earnings from April 20-24 includes Tesla, Intel, UnitedHealth Group, and GE Aerospace, potentially influencing sentiment in tech, healthcare, and industrials sectors. Results could set market tone amid geopolitical jitters.[2]

items.title: Wells Fargo CEO Stresses Fed Independence Amid Trump Comments
items.summary: Wells Fargo CEO Charlie Scharf emphasized Federal Reserve independence as 'critically important' while noting President Trump can voice his views, in a Monday interview at the Economic Club of Washington DC.[4][6]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Kelp DAO Suffers $300M Exploit Triggering $9B Panic Withdrawal from Aave
items.summary: Hackers exploited Kelp DAO's bridge via LayerZero, stealing 116,500 rsETH worth nearly $300M, leading to $9B outflows from Aave and slashing its TVL by over a third to $17.5B. Kelp paused the protocol 46 minutes after the attack on Sunday.

items.title: Crypto Market Dips 0.81% to $2.53T Amid US-Iran Tensions, BTC Below $75K
items.summary: Total crypto market cap fell 0.81% in 24 hours but up 5% weekly; Bitcoin dropped to ~$74K-75K (-1.11%), Ethereum below $2,300 (-2.37%), linked to stock market reactions to US-Iran clashes. Top performers: SUSHI (+2.5%), IOTA (+2%), NEAR (+2%).

items.title: Spot BTC ETFs See $996M Inflows, ETH ETFs $276M Weekly as Market Pulls Back
items.summary: Net inflows into spot Bitcoin ETFs reached $996.4M, while US spot Ethereum ETFs recorded $275.8M weekly; BTC hash rate recovered to 992 EH/s despite price retreat viewed as uptrend pullback.

items.title: SocialFi and AI Sectors Buck Downtrend with Gains Amid Broader Market Dip
items.summary: SocialFi rose 1.92% (TON +1.49%, CHZ +4.60%), AI up 0.95% (PIEVERSE +77.43%, VVV +4.89%); other sectors like DeFi (-2.24%), Layer 2 (-2.28%) declined due to macro factors.

items.title: $1.4B Flows into Crypto Funds in Largest Weekly Total Since Early Year
items.summary: Crypto investment funds recorded $1.4B inflows, the biggest weekly total since early in the year, amid ongoing market volatility and ETF demand resurgence.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google Plans Release of New AI Chips to Challenge Nvidia
items.summary: Google is preparing to launch specialized AI chips focused on inference, separate from training chips, amid growing demand and leveraging its TPUs for models like Gemini. This move aims to compete directly with Nvidia in the AI hardware market.

items.title: Leaks Suggest GPT-5.5 from OpenAI Shows Substantial Performance Jumps
items.summary: Recent leaks on Twitter indicate GPT-5.5 could one-shot many applications and close the gap with leading models like Anthropic's Opus 4.7, potentially allowing OpenAI to reclaim the top spot in AI benchmarks.

items.title: Gen Z Expresses Growing Anger and Fears Over AI Job Displacement
items.summary: Gen Z influencers highlight a shift from excitement to concern about AI, with escalating fears of job losses as the younger generation sours on the technology.

items.title: Adobe CEO Addresses AI Impacts Amid Stock Concerns
items.summary: Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen discusses the company's AI integrations and counters bearish sentiments on software stocks driven by AI-related worries.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Neverwinter Announces Module 33 First Look, New Event, and Roadmap Tease
items.summary: Neverwinter revealed a first look at Module 33 this week, including a new ice-themed zone teaser, trial loot improvements with reroll tokens, arena buffs, bug fixes, and a better-than-recruitment event. A roadmap reveal is expected tomorrow alongside today's patch notes.

items.title: Multiple Unannounced Nintendo Switch 2 Games Rated Online
items.summary: Several unannounced Switch 2 games have appeared in online ratings, including mystery titles from another company, alongside rumors of Assassin's Creed Black Flag remaster reveal this week.

items.title: Valve Hints at Steam Machine Update and New Steam Controller
items.summary: Documents suggest Valve is updating the Steam Machine, with mentions of a new Steam controller and large quantity shipments, as per insider Brad Lynch.

items.title: Indie Game Elementallis Finally Gets Release Date Next Week
items.summary: After years of silence, indie game Elementallis has a confirmed release date just a week from tomorrow, highlighted in the latest indie gaming roundup for April 20-26.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Blue Origin's New Glenn Rocket Grounded After Failed Launch
items.summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket launch failed due to a bad engine, placing a satellite in the wrong orbit and grounding the vehicle. The company is investigating the anomaly that doomed the mission.

items.title: Google Announces New AI Inference Chips to Challenge Nvidia
items.summary: Google is introducing new chips dedicated to AI inference to compete with Nvidia in the fast-growing semiconductor market for running AI models. The TPUs are gaining traction among AI developers, including rivals.
